ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
aril: noun. A tissue surrounding the seed in certain fruits such as pomegranates.
aulic: adjective. Of or pertaining to a royal court; courtly.
auric: adjective. Of or pertaining to trivalent gold.
car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
carl: noun. A rude, rustic man; a churl.
carr: noun. Any marsh; marshy ground, swampland.
caul: noun. A style of close-fitting circular cap worn by women in the sixteenth century and later, often made of linen.
circular: adjective. Of or relating to a circle.
crucial: adjective. Essential or decisive for determining the outcome or future of something; extremely important; vital.
cur: noun. A contemptible or inferior dog.
curia: noun. Any of the subdivisions of a tribe in ancient Rome
curial: adjective. Pertaining to a court; courtly.
curl: noun. A curving piece or lock of hair; a ringlet.
curr: verb. To coo like an owl.
lac: noun. A resinous substance or lacquer produced mainly on the banyan tree by the female of Kerria lacca, a scale insect.
laic: noun. A layperson as opposed to a cleric, i.e. not a member of the clergy.
lair: noun. A place inhabited by a wild animal, often a cave or a hole in the ground.
lar: noun. singular of lares: a household god, particularly overseeing the family itself.
lari: noun. The national currency of Georgia, divided into 100 tetri.
liar: noun. Someone who tells a lie; especially, a person who frequently lies.
lira: noun. The basic unit of currency in Turkey.
rail: noun. A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rial: noun. The official currencies of Iran, Oman, and Yemen.
rural: adjective. Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
uracil: noun. One of the bases of RNA, pairing with adenine.
urial: noun. A bearded reddish sheep, subspecies of Ovis orientalis (including Ovis orientalis vignei), previously classified as Ovis vignei,
uric: adjective. Pertaining to, contained in, or obtained from urine.
aru: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arua.
aul: noun. A village encampment in the Caucasus, Central Asia or the Southern Urals.
crc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lonwolwol.
crl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern East Cree.
cru: noun. A vineyard or group of vineyards in France, especially one having a high reputation.
cul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Culina.
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lauric: adjective. Pertaining to, or derived from, the European bay or laurel (Laurus nobilis)
lur: noun. A long natural blowing horn without finger holes, played by embouchure.
rauli: noun. Nothofagus alpina, a deciduous tree of Chile and Argentina.
urlar: noun. The base theme of a piece of classical bagpipe music.
